evals.name: Angular 19 standalone scaffold completeness
evals.prompt: Scaffold an Angular 19 enterprise app with standalone components, Signals-based state, functional auth guard, functional HTTP interceptor, lazy routes with loadComponent, and Angular Material.
evals.expected_output: Complete scaffold with app.config.ts, app.routes.ts, auth service with Signals, functional guard, functional interceptor, and example dashboard component

assertions.text: Output includes app.config.ts with provideRouter and provideHttpClient
Output includes functional authGuard using inject()
Output includes functional HttpInterceptorFn interceptor
Output uses signal() and computed() for state in services
Output uses loadComponent for lazy standalone routes
Output uses @if and @for control flow, NOT *ngIf or *ngFor
Output does NOT create NgModules for new components
Output uses inject() function, not constructor injection
Output includes standalone: true in all component metadata

evals.name: BehaviorSubject to Signals migration
evals.prompt: Migrate this service from BehaviorSubject to Signals: a UserService with private _user$ = new BehaviorSubject<User | null>(null), user$ = this._user$.asObservable(), setUser(u: User) { this._user$.next(u) }
evals.expected_output: Migrated service using signal(), asReadonly(), and any needed computed() — with notes on template updates

assertions.text: Output replaces BehaviorSubject with signal()
Output uses asReadonly() to expose signal publicly
Output uses .set() instead of .next()
Output does NOT use BehaviorSubject or Subject for state
Output notes that templates using async pipe need updating

evals.name: Class guard to functional guard migration
evals.prompt: Convert this class-based CanActivate guard to a functional guard: class AuthGuard implements CanActivate { constructor(private auth: AuthService, private router: Router) {} canActivate() { return this.auth.isLoggedIn ? true : this.router.createUrlTree(['/login']) } }
evals.expected_output: Functional guard using inject() that returns true or UrlTree

assertions.text: Output does NOT use class implementing CanActivate
Output uses inject(AuthService) and inject(Router)
Output returns boolean or UrlTree
Output is a plain function, not a class

evals.name: Template control flow modernization
evals.prompt:
Update this template from structural directives to Angular 17+ built-in control flow:
  • {{ item.name }}

assertions.text: Output uses @if and @else blocks
Output uses @for with track clause
Output does NOT use *ngIf
Output does NOT use *ngFor
Output does NOT use ng-template with structural directives

evals.name: NgRx Signals Store for cross-feature state
evals.prompt: Create an NgRx Signals Store for a shopping cart with: items array, add/remove methods, computed itemCount and total price, and providedIn: root.
evals.expected_output: signalStore() with withState, withComputed, withMethods, providedIn root

assertions.text: Output uses signalStore() from @ngrx/signals
Output uses withState() for initial state
Output uses withComputed() for itemCount and total
Output uses withMethods() for add and remove
Output includes { providedIn: 'root' } option
Output does NOT use classic NgRx actions/reducers/effects pattern
